mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off repricing that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power for short-term drawdowns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in housing starts and employment growth confirms a broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing corporate revenue visibility and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample backtests, or forward returns show no statistical difference between high-high and low-low proxy combinations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase indicates capacity utilization normalization, which historically precedes earnings upgrades and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are flat or negative when INDPRO rises during early-cycle phases, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and producing positive forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or quarterly forward returns show no significant premium during GDP acceleration periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly consumer price index increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers market fears of premature monetary tightening, compressing equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral despite repeated CPI spikes above the threshold, or the signal generates excessive false positives in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Diverging labor market metrics where employment growth slows while the unemployment rate remains sticky indicate structural labor friction, reducing corporate margin expectations and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over individual series, or forward returns do not differ significantly between divergence and convergence regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in residential investment during a credit cycle peak signals impending demand destruction, prompting immediate equity de-risking and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive despite RSAFS declines, or the signal fails to predict short-term drawdowns when housing investment falls outside peak credit conditions.
